mappings.reason: Restricting administrator privileges to dedicated accounts ensures that only authorized personnel possess the elevated rights required to modify system time data, satisfying the access restriction objective for administrative configurations.

Defining and maintaining role-based access control establishes the authorization framework that assigns time modification rights only to specific authorized roles, directly supporting the restriction to authorized personnel.
Managing and disabling default accounts removes unauthorized access vectors that could otherwise permit time configuration changes, contributing to the restriction of access to authorized personnel.
Centralizing network AAA enforces authorization controls on network infrastructure devices, which often serve as time sources, restricting configuration access on that subset of enterprise assets.
